title: Emergency, Doctor, Pharmacy
description: You call for help, check in at the hospital, talk to a doctor, and understand medicine instructions.
context: Something happens and you need help. You call emergency services, check in at the hospital, explain symptoms to the doctor, and then get medicine at the pharmacy.

exchanges.speaker: dispatcher
exchanges.text: Tiesňová linka 112, dobrý deň. Čo sa stalo?
exchanges.translation: Emergency line 112, good day. What happened?
exchanges.prompt: The dispatcher asks what happened. Someone is injured. What do you say first?

choices.text: Je to núdza! Niekto je zranený!
choices.translation: It is an emergency! Someone is injured!

exchanges.correctFeedback: Je to núdza! Niekto je zranený! — correct. Say the emergency clearly first.
exchanges.wrongFeedback: In an emergency, start clearly: Je to núdza! Niekto je zranený!

exchanges.speaker: dispatcher
exchanges.translation: Do you need an ambulance, police, or firefighters?
exchanges.prompt: The person is injured and needs medical help. What do you ask for?
exchanges.correctFeedback: Pošlite sanitku! — correct for an injured person who needs medical help.
exchanges.wrongFeedback: For an injured person, ask for an ambulance: Pošlite sanitku!

exchanges.speaker: dispatcher
exchanges.text: Záchranár je na ceste. Zostaňte pri zranenom človeku.
exchanges.translation: A paramedic is on the way. Stay with the injured person.
exchanges.prompt: The dispatcher says a paramedic is on the way. What do you say?

choices.translation: I will wait for the paramedic.
I am going to the pharmacy.

exchanges.correctFeedback: Počkám na záchranára. — correct. You confirm you will wait with the injured person.
exchanges.wrongFeedback: Tell the dispatcher you will wait: Počkám na záchranára.

exchanges.speaker: receptionist
exchanges.text: Dobrý deň. Ste objednaný alebo bez objednania?
exchanges.translation: Good day. Do you have an appointment or are you without one?
exchanges.prompt: You came urgently without an appointment. What do you say?

choices.text: Som bez objednania. Je to urgentné.
choices.translation: I am without an appointment. It is urgent.

exchanges.correctFeedback: Som bez objednania. Je to urgentné. — correct hospital check-in phrase for urgent care.
exchanges.wrongFeedback: If you came urgently without an appointment, say Som bez objednania. Je to urgentné.

exchanges.speaker: receptionist
exchanges.prompt: Reception asks for your insurance card. What do you give?
exchanges.correctFeedback: Tu je moja karta poistenca. — correct. Karta poistenca is the insurance card.
exchanges.wrongFeedback: Reception is asking for karta poistenca — insurance card.

exchanges.speaker: doctor
exchanges.translation: Good day. What is troubling you?
exchanges.prompt: The doctor asks what is wrong. Pick a symptom sentence.
exchanges.correctFeedback: Correct. Mám + symptom and Je mi zle are key medical phrases.
exchanges.wrongFeedback: Use Mám + symptom, or Je mi zle, to explain symptoms.

exchanges.speaker: doctor
exchanges.translation: How long has this been going on?
exchanges.prompt: It started yesterday. What do you say?
exchanges.correctFeedback: Od včera. — correct. Od + time tells the doctor since when.
exchanges.wrongFeedback: Use Od včera when symptoms started yesterday.

exchanges.speaker: doctor
exchanges.text: Dám vám predpis. Berte tento liek a oddychujte.
exchanges.translation: I will give you a prescription. Take this medicine and rest.
exchanges.prompt: The doctor gives you a prescription. What do you say?

choices.translation: Thank you. I am going to the pharmacy.

exchanges.correctFeedback: Ďakujem. Idem do lekárne. — correct. You thank the doctor and head to the pharmacy.
exchanges.wrongFeedback: Thank the doctor and say where you are going: Ďakujem. Idem do lekárne.

exchanges.speaker: pharmacist
exchanges.text: Dobrý deň. Ako vám môžem pomôcť?
exchanges.translation: Good day. How can I help you?
exchanges.prompt: You are at the pharmacy and have a prescription. What do you say?

choices.translation: I have a prescription from the doctor.
I live on the ground floor.

exchanges.correctFeedback: Mám predpis od doktora. — correct. The pharmacist knows you have a prescription.
exchanges.wrongFeedback: At the pharmacy with a prescription, say Mám predpis od doktora.

exchanges.speaker: pharmacist
exchanges.text: Tu sú tabletky. Užívajte ich dvakrát denne pred jedlom.
exchanges.translation: Here are the pills. Take them twice a day before meals.
exchanges.prompt: The pharmacist gives medicine instructions. Which response confirms the instruction?

choices.translation: Twice a day, before meals. I understand.

exchanges.correctFeedback: Dvakrát denne, pred jedlom. Rozumiem. — correct. Repeating instructions helps avoid mistakes.
exchanges.wrongFeedback: The instruction is dvakrát denne, pred jedlom — twice daily before meals.

exchanges.speaker: pharmacist
exchanges.prompt: The pharmacist asks if you need anything else. You are done. What do you say?
exchanges.correctFeedback: Nie, ďakujem. — correct. A polite way to end the conversation.
exchanges.wrongFeedback: When you are done at the pharmacy, say Nie, ďakujem.

exchanges.speaker: pharmacist
exchanges.prompt: The pharmacist says goodbye. How do you respond?
exchanges.correctFeedback: Ďakujem. Dovidenia! — correct. A warm farewell to close the conversation.
exchanges.wrongFeedback: Reply to the farewell with Ďakujem. Dovidenia!

completionMessage: 🎉 Great job! You called for help, identified emergency services, checked in at the hospital, explained symptoms, got a prescription, and understood pharmacy instructions in Slovak.
